name: analytics
description:
Analyze locally synced data with count, group-by, and summary operations.
Data must be synced first with the sync command.

limit.description: Max groups to show (default: 25)

name: annotations_export
description: Export annotations as markdown or JSON

collection.description: Scope to items in this collection key

refresh.description: Fetch live from the API instead of the local store (default: false)

tag.description: Scope to items with this tag

name: annotations_timeline
description: List annotations sorted by creation date

collection.description: Scope to items in this collection key

item.description: Scope to annotations of a specific parent item key

limit.description: Maximum number of annotations to return (default: 50)

refresh.description: Fetch live from the API instead of the local store (default: false)

since.description: ISO date string; include annotations after this date

name: attachments_add
description:
Attach a local file (typically a PDF) to an existing item through the
Zotero Web API. Mode "stored" uploads an imported_file child that syncs to all
devices. Mode "linked-file" records the absolute local path without consuming
Zotero storage quota; the bytes remain local to this machine.

Both modes are retry-safe. Stored files reconcile by filename and registered
MD5. Linked files reconcile by absolute path. An identical retry no-ops instead
of creating another child.

By default this previews the planned attachment; apply with --yes.

allow-destructive.description: Allow irreversible operations (merge, permanent delete, empty-trash) to apply (default: false)

args.description: Additional positional arguments to append to the command. Raw CLI flags are rejected.

continue-on-error.description: On bulk mutations, continue past per-item failures/conflicts instead of stopping at the first (default: false)

max-changes.description: Max write operations a single mutation may apply before refusing (-1 = default: 500, or 50 under --agent) (default: -1)

max-failures.description: With --continue-on-error, stop after this many failures (0 = unlimited) (default: 0)

mode.description: Attachment handling: stored (synced) or linked-file (local path) (default: stored)

yes.description: Skip confirmation prompts (for agents and scripts) (default: false)

name: capabilities
description:
Outputs a typed registry describing each command's operation kind
(read/write/destructive), write target, supported data sources, and
preconditions (live_local_api, web_api_key, synced_store, better_bibtex) so
agents can select safe commands and pre-flight requirements without parsing
--help or guessing from names.

pretty.description: indent JSON output for human reading (default: false)

name: collections_bundle
description:
Assemble a self-contained research package from the local store for a
collection: synthesis context, annotations, and compact bibliography. This command
never writes to Zotero; run sync first if local data is missing.

args.description: Additional positional arguments to append to the command. Raw CLI flags are rejected.

out.description: Output directory for the research package

name: context
description: Get API domain context: resource taxonomy, auth requirements, query tips, and unique capabilities. Call this first.

name: creators_audit
description:
Audit synced creator names for variant candidates without mutating Zotero.

The audit reads item creators from the local synced store and groups likely name
variants into three confidence tiers: exact-after-normalization, initial/full-name
compatibility, and ambiguous same-surname diagnostics. Use --orcid to fetch
CrossRef author ORCIDs for DOI-bearing tier-2/3 candidates and store them in the
local creator_orcids sidecar table as corroboration evidence. That sidecar is
local-only evidence; zotio never writes ORCIDs back to Zotero because Zotero has
no creator ORCID field.

orcid.description: Fetch CrossRef author ORCIDs into the local-only sidecar table; never writes ORCIDs to Zotero (default: false)

scope.description: Item scope: library, collection:<key>, tag:<tag>, item:<key>, or query:<text> (default: library)

name: creators_audit_fix
description:
Apply safe creator variant renames from creators audit through the shared
mutation preview/apply envelope.

By default this previews item writes without sending network mutations. Pass --yes
to apply. Tier-1 exact-normalized groups are planned automatically. Tier-2
initial/full-name groups are planned only for explicit repeatable mappings such
as --map "J. Smith=John Smith". Tier-3 ambiguous groups are never planned.

Creator renames PATCH each affected item with its full creators array and a
version precondition, preserving creatorType, creator order, and unrelated
creators. Applied creator renames are journaled for audit history, but journal
undo does not reverse them because ordered creator-array inverses are not
supported.

allow-destructive.description: Allow irreversible operations (merge, permanent delete, empty-trash) to apply (default: false)

continue-on-error.description: On bulk mutations, continue past per-item failures/conflicts instead of stopping at the first (default: false)

max-changes.description: Max write operations a single mutation may apply before refusing (-1 = default: 500, or 50 under --agent) (default: -1)

max-failures.description: With --continue-on-error, stop after this many failures (0 = unlimited) (default: 0)

scope.description: Item scope: library, collection:<key>, tag:<tag>, item:<key>, or query:<text> (default: library)

yes.description: Skip confirmation prompts (for agents and scripts) (default: false)

name: demo
description:
Seed a bundled sample library into a separate demo store and print a
short tour of commands to try.

The sandbox is a self-contained SQLite database (demo.db) beside your real
store. Set ZOTIO_DEMO=1 on any command to read from it with a pristine,
key-less config -- your real library, config, and API key are never touched.
No Zotero desktop and no API key are required.

reset.description: Delete and re-seed the sandbox (also removes demo.db) (default: false)

name: export
description:
Export paginated API data to a local file. Supports JSONL (one JSON object
per line, streaming-friendly) and JSON (array). JSONL is recommended for
large datasets as it has no memory pressure.

allow-destructive.description: Allow irreversible operations (merge, permanent delete, empty-trash) to apply (default: false)

args.description: Additional positional arguments to append to the command. Raw CLI flags are rejected.

continue-on-error.description: On bulk mutations, continue past per-item failures/conflicts instead of stopping at the first (default: false)

format.description: Output format: jsonl or json (default: jsonl)

limit.description: Maximum records to export (0 = unlimited) (default: 0)

max-changes.description: Max write operations a single mutation may apply before refusing (-1 = default: 500, or 50 under --agent) (default: -1)

max-failures.description: With --continue-on-error, stop after this many failures (0 = unlimited) (default: 0)

no-cache.description: Bypass response cache for fresh data (default: false)

yes.description: Skip confirmation prompts (for agents and scripts) (default: false)

name: export_snapshot
description:
Export a structured item set (JSONL) across all pages into a data file,
plus a sidecar lockfile (<output>.lock.json) recording each item's key+version
and a content hash for reproducibility/drift detection. Resumable: an interrupted
run can continue with --resume (a <output>.checkpoint.json sidecar tracks progress).

Scope is one of: library (default), collection:KEY, or tag:NAME.

args.description: Additional positional arguments to append to the command. Raw CLI flags are rejected.

limit.description: Maximum items to export (0 = all) (default: 0)

output.description: Output JSONL data file (required); the lockfile is written to <output>.lock.json

resume.description: Resume an interrupted snapshot from its checkpoint sidecar (default: false)

name: export_snapshot_verify
description:
Verify a snapshot lockfile against the current Zotero library.

The verifier re-resolves the lockfile scope when possible, recomputes the same
content hash as export snapshot, and separates semantic drift (added, removed,
changed) from Zotero version churn (touched). Touched items have a newer version
but identical normalized content and never fail --fail-on-drift.

args.description: Additional positional arguments to append to the command. Raw CLI flags are rejected.

fail-on-drift.description: Exit 11 when added, removed, or changed items are detected (touched never fails) (default: false)

name: import_pdf
description:
Create Zotero items from local PDF files by asking the Zotero desktop Connector
API to save each PDF as a standalone attachment and run Zotero's metadata recognizer.

This command requires a local Zotero base URL, Zotero running, and Zotero's local API
preference enabled. Recognition failures are reported as unrecognized standalone PDF
attachments instead of hard errors.

args.description: Additional positional arguments to append to the command. Raw CLI flags are rejected.

name: import_resolve
description: Resolve PDFs into an editable import manifest

args.description: Additional positional arguments to append to the command. Raw CLI flags are rejected.

limit.description: Scan at most N PDFs when resolving a directory (default: 200)

name: import_scan
description:
Scan a directory of PDFs and classify each against your synced library WITHOUT
importing anything. For each PDF it extracts a DOI (from the filename, then the
file's embedded metadata) and reports:

  duplicate         the DOI already belongs to an item in your library
  attach_candidate  matches an item you have that is missing its PDF
  new               not in your library (use 'import doi <DOI>' to add it)
  unidentified      no DOI found (rename the file with its DOI, or add it in Zotero)

This complements Zotero's "Retrieve Metadata for PDF": it makes the library-aware
decision the GUI does not — which PDFs to import, which are duplicates, and which
complete items you already have. It never writes; hand the actual import and file
attach to Zotero.

DOI extraction is dependency-free: it reads the filename and the PDF's uncompressed
embedded metadata (Info/XMP). It does NOT decode compressed page text, so scanned
or text-only PDFs may report "unidentified".

args.description: Additional positional arguments to append to the command. Raw CLI flags are rejected.

limit.description: Scan at most N PDFs (0 = all) (default: 0)

resolve.description: Fetch CrossRef titles for 'new' PDFs (network) (default: false)

name: import_targets
description:
List Zotero desktop Connector save targets for connector-backed imports.
Use the id column (for example C78) with --connector-target when --collection is
ambiguous or the local Web API collection key cannot be mapped automatically.

name: import_translators
description:
Fetch a page and ask Zotero desktop which web translators match it.

This is diagnostic-only: Zotero's desktop connector can detect matching web
translators, but browser-side web translation is not run by the desktop server.

args.description: Additional positional arguments to append to the command. Raw CLI flags are rejected.

name: import_url
description:
Import a URL, resolving real metadata where possible.

If the URL embeds a DOI, full metadata is fetched from CrossRef. Otherwise the
page's embedded metadata (citation_*, Open Graph, Dublin Core meta tags) is
mapped into a typed item with title, creators, abstract, and publication venue.
A bare webpage item is used only when no metadata is available.

Use --dry-run to preview the proposed item without writing it.

allow-destructive.description: Allow irreversible operations (merge, permanent delete, empty-trash) to apply (default: false)

args.description: Additional positional arguments to append to the command. Raw CLI flags are rejected.

collection.description: Collection key to add the item to

continue-on-error.description: On bulk mutations, continue past per-item failures/conflicts instead of stopping at the first (default: false)

max-failures.description: With --continue-on-error, stop after this many failures (0 = unlimited) (default: 0)

yes.description: Skip confirmation prompts (for agents and scripts) (default: false)

name: init
description:
Guided first-run setup for Zotero automation.

Checks the local Zotero API, stores an API key when one is provided interactively,
runs the first local sync when the store is missing or empty, finishes with the quick
library-health preset, and offers an opt-in daily check for public zotio releases.
The check sends only a GitHub releases request and no identifying payload.

allow-destructive.description: Allow irreversible operations (merge, permanent delete, empty-trash) to apply (default: false)

continue-on-error.description: On bulk mutations, continue past per-item failures/conflicts instead of stopping at the first (default: false)

launch.description: Launch Zotero and wait for the local API when it is not reachable (default: false)

max-changes.description: Max write operations a single mutation may apply before refusing (-1 = default: 500, or 50 under --agent) (default: -1)

max-failures.description: With --continue-on-error, stop after this many failures (0 = unlimited) (default: 0)

name: items_bibliography
description:
Render a formatted bibliography for items selected with the shared scope grammar.

The bibliography is rendered by Zotero's Web API CSL renderer so named styles
such as apa, chicago, mla, nature, and journal-specific CSL IDs are honored.
The Web API limits itemKey batches, so large scopes are fetched in stable
50-key chunks and concatenated in scope order.

scope.description: Shared scope expression (library, collection:KEY, tag:NAME, item:KEY, query:TEXT) (default: library)

style.description: CSL style ID (default uses Zotero's default bibliography style)

name: items_cite
description:
Retrieve a formatted bibliographic citation for an item. The Zotero local
API renders citations using your installed CSL styles. For the most common
styles (apa, chicago, mla), use the --style flag. For others, pass the
CSL style ID directly (e.g. "nature", "harvard-cite-them-right").

args.description: Additional positional arguments to append to the command. Raw CLI flags are rejected.

style.description: Citation style (apa, mla, chicago, bibtex, ris, csljson) — default uses Zotero's current default style

name: items_duplicates_resolve
description:
Resolve duplicate items by merging metadata onto a master and trashing duplicates.

By default, resolve only matches duplicate DOI groups. Title matching can group
distinct items that share generic titles; pass --title only when you are ready to
review the preview carefully before applying.

allow-destructive.description: Allow irreversible operations (merge, permanent delete, empty-trash) to apply (default: false)

continue-on-error.description: On bulk mutations, continue past per-item failures/conflicts instead of stopping at the first (default: false)

title.description: Opt into riskier duplicate title groups (default: false)

yes.description: Skip confirmation prompts (for agents and scripts) (default: false)

name: items_enrich
description:
Resolve missing metadata for locally synced items and apply it back to Zotero.

Work queues come from the same checks as 'items audit':
  --missing-doi       resolve a DOI by title from CrossRef, then OpenAlex/Semantic Scholar (exact title match)
  --missing-abstract  fill the abstract from CrossRef, then OpenAlex/Semantic Scholar (requires the item's DOI)
  --missing-pdf       attach an open-access PDF from Unpaywall (requires DOI)

PDF attachment modes:
  linked-url   create a linked_url attachment (default; no download)
  linked-file  download the PDF to --pdf-dir and create a linked_file child item; linked files do not sync to other devices

Downloaded PDFs accept application/pdf, application/octet-stream, or an absent
Content-Type, then must pass a %PDF- magic check. Stored (imported-file)
retro-attachment is handled by 'zotio attachments add <item-key> <file>', which
uploads through the Zotero Web API file-upload protocol.

By default this previews the proposed changes (a patch plan) and never downloads
PDF bytes. Pass --collection to scope the work queue to items in a single
collection, or --keys/--keys-from to scope to exact item keys. Pass --yes to
apply via the Zotero API; --dry-run always previews.
Applied field changes record provenance in the item's Extra field.

allow-destructive.description: Allow irreversible operations (merge, permanent delete, empty-trash) to apply (default: false)

attach-mode.description: PDF attachment handling: linked-url or linked-file; stored retro-attachment is handled by `zotio attachments add` (default: linked-url)

collection.description: Scope the work queue to items in a collection key

continue-on-error.description: On bulk mutations, continue past per-item failures/conflicts instead of stopping at the first (default: false)

email.description: Contact email for Unpaywall (required for --missing-pdf) and the OpenAlex polite pool (optional); or set UNPAYWALL_EMAIL

keys.description: Comma-separated exact item keys to enrich

keys-from.description: Read exact item keys from a file or '-' for stdin, then enrich only matching queued items

limit.description: Maximum items to process per category (default: 25)

max-changes.description: Max write operations a single mutation may apply before refusing (-1 = default: 500, or 50 under --agent) (default: -1)

max-failures.description: With --continue-on-error, stop after this many failures (0 = unlimited) (default: 0)

missing-abstract.description: Fill the abstract from CrossRef, OpenAlex, or Semantic Scholar (uses the item's DOI) (default: false)

missing-doi.description: Resolve and add a DOI from CrossRef, OpenAlex, or Semantic Scholar (default: false)

missing-pdf.description: Attach an open-access PDF from Unpaywall as a link or download (uses the item's DOI) (default: false)

no-openalex.description: Disable the OpenAlex fallback for --missing-doi/--missing-abstract (default: false)

no-semantic-scholar.description: Disable the Semantic Scholar fallback for --missing-doi/--missing-abstract (default: false)

pdf-dir.description: Directory for linked-file PDF downloads; responses must be PDF/octet-stream/unspecified Content-Type plus %PDF- magic

validate.description: Read-only DOI discrepancy report against CrossRef and OpenCitations (default: false)

yes.description: Skip confirmation prompts (for agents and scripts) (default: false)

name: items_file
description:
Return the local filesystem location of an item's attachment via the Zotero
local API (/items/<key>/file/view/url). Accepts an attachment key directly, or a
regular item key whose PDF attachment is resolved automatically through its child
items.

This complements 'items open' (which launches the Zotero desktop app): 'items file'
hands you the actual file so an agent or script can read or process it. The local
API must be enabled (Settings → Advanced → "Allow other applications…").

Check DOI-bearing items from the locally synced store against CrossRef's
updated-by metadata. The command reports retractions, expressions of concern,
and corrections without writing to Zotero. Run 'zotio sync' first so the local
store is current.

name: items_similar
description: Rank locally similar items using five weighted signals: collections (0.30), tags (0.25), fulltext rare-word overlap (0.25), creators (0.10), and an exact, binary venue match (0.10). Run 'zotio sync' first to populate the local mirror and 'zotio sync --fulltext' to include the fulltext signal. --min-score filters the resulting weighted composite score.

name: items_summarize
description:
Gather the highest-signal local context for an item (or every item in a
collection) into one bounded bundle — citation, abstract, your annotations, a
capped fulltext excerpt, and known metadata gaps — plus a synthesis prompt.

This command never calls a model: it does the assembly and budgeting the host LLM
is bad at, then hands off. Reads are local only. With --agent/--json it emits the
structured bundle; otherwise a readable Markdown brief you can paste into any LLM.

name: library_health
description:
Run a ranked, finding-typed health report over the locally synced library.

Composes the existing audit primitives (citekey conflicts, duplicates, missing
metadata, tag drift, broken attachments) into one report. Pick what "ready" means
with --for:

  --for citation           manuscript/bibliography readiness (citekeys, core fields, duplicates)
  --for systematic-review  PRISMA screening corpus (duplicates, screenable metadata, full text)
  --for quick   (default)  anything obviously broken (citekey conflicts, duplicates, attachments)
  --for all                every check

Gate CI with --fail-on critical|high|info (or legacy any) (exit 11 when the bar is not met).

The broken-attachment and retraction checks are live checks that need Zotero
desktop or CrossRef network access respectively; pass --verify-files and/or
--check-retractions to run them. When a gate-relevant check can't run because
its precondition is unmet, the command refuses loudly (exit 9) rather than passing.

badge.description: Emit a shields.io endpoint JSON badge instead of the report (default: false)

badge-label.description: Label for the shields.io endpoint badge (default: bibliography)

baseline.description: Read health finding identities from this baseline JSON; missing file establishes a baseline

check-retractions.description: Run the live CrossRef retraction check (network; DOI-bearing items) (default: false)

fail-on.description: Exit 11 if findings reach this severity: critical, high, info/any; none disables the gate (default: the preset's)

fail-on-new.description: Exit 11 if new baseline-diff findings reach this severity: critical, high, info/any (requires --baseline)

for.description: Check preset: quick, citation, systematic-review, all (default: quick)

limit.description: Max findings listed per kind (0 = all); also caps the live attachment scan (default: 0)

report.description: Write the full JSON health report to this file in addition to stdout/badge output

require-fresh.description: Refuse (exit 12) when the local store is staler than this (e.g. 24h); 0 = disabled (default: 0s)

scope.description: Limit to a cohort: collection:KEY | tag:NAME | item:KEY | query:TEXT | saved-search:KEY (default: whole library)

verify-files.description: Run the live broken-attachment check (needs Zotero desktop running) (default: false)

write-baseline.description: Write current health finding identities to this baseline JSON after checks

name: library_prisma
description:
Report the PRISMA 2020 identification-stage counts for a locally synced screening corpus.

The report counts records identified (including their source databases), duplicate
records removed, and the records-after-deduplication input to screening. Screening
itself is deliberately out of scope: use Rayyan, ASReview, or another screening tool
for that stage. Run 'zotio items duplicates resolve' to actually remove duplicates
before exporting the certified corpus.

by.description: Duplicate detector to run (doi, title, all) (default: all)

scope.description: Limit to library, collection:<key>, tag:<name>, item:<key>, or query:<text>

name: sql
description: Run read-only SQL against local database. Use for ad-hoc analysis, aggregations, and joins across synced resources. Requires sync first. Returns a JSON object with rows, truncated, and row_limit; large responses are bounded to the MCP tool-result budget.

query.description: SQL query (SELECT or WITH...SELECT). Tables match resource names.

name: sync
description:
Sync data from the API into a local SQLite database. Supports resumable
incremental sync (only fetches new data since last sync) and full resync.
Once synced, use the 'search' command for instant full-text search.

Exit codes & warnings:
  Resources the API denies access to (HTTP 403, or HTTP 400 with an
  access-policy body) are reported as warnings rather than failing the
  run. In --json mode each is emitted as a {"event":"sync_warning",...}
  line carrying status, reason, and message fields, and a final
  {"event":"sync_summary",...} aggregates the run.

  Exit 0 when at least one resource synced and no resource flagged in
  the spec as critical (x-critical: true) failed. Pass --strict to exit
  non-zero on any per-resource failure. Exit is always
  non-zero when every selected resource failed, regardless of --strict.

allow-destructive.description: Allow irreversible operations (merge, permanent delete, empty-trash) to apply (default: false)

continue-on-error.description: On bulk mutations, continue past per-item failures/conflicts instead of stopping at the first (default: false)

fulltext.description: Also sync PDF full-text content (slower; one request per attachment) (default: false)

latest-only.description: Refresh head of each resource only; clears resume cursor and caps pages at 1. Mutually exclusive with --since (--since wins). (default: false)

max-changes.description: Max write operations a single mutation may apply before refusing (-1 = default: 500, or 50 under --agent) (default: -1)

max-failures.description: With --continue-on-error, stop after this many failures (0 = unlimited) (default: 0)

max-pages.description: Maximum pages to fetch per resource (0 = unlimited; cap-hit emits a sync_warning event) (default: 100)

resources.description: Comma-separated resource types to sync (selecting items also syncs items-trash)

since.description: Only sync objects modified since this Zotero library version (0 = use stored checkpoint). Get versions from a prior sync or 'items list --since'. (default: 0)

strict.description: Exit non-zero on any per-resource failure (default: only critical failures or all-resource failure exit non-zero). (default: false)

yes.description: Skip confirmation prompts (for agents and scripts) (default: false)

name: tags_audit
description: Audit tags for case and spacing drift

name: tags_audit_fix
description: Apply the tag rename plan produced by tags audit

allow-destructive.description: Allow irreversible operations (merge, permanent delete, empty-trash) to apply (default: false)

continue-on-error.description: On bulk mutations, continue past per-item failures/conflicts instead of stopping at the first (default: false)

max-changes.description: Max write operations a single mutation may apply before refusing (-1 = default: 500, or 50 under --agent) (default: -1)

max-failures.description: With --continue-on-error, stop after this many failures (0 = unlimited) (default: 0)

yes.description: Skip confirmation prompts (for agents and scripts) (default: false)

name: tags_get
description: Get a specific tag by name

args.description: Additional positional arguments to append to the command. Raw CLI flags are rejected.

name: tags_inventory
description: Show tag usage within collections and across the library

collection.description: Show only tags within this collection key

name: tags_list
description: List all tags in the library

limit.description: Maximum number of tags to return (default: 0)

name: tags_rename
description: Rename a tag across matching items

allow-destructive.description: Allow irreversible operations (merge, permanent delete, empty-trash) to apply (default: false)

args.description: Additional positional arguments to append to the command. Raw CLI flags are rejected.

continue-on-error.description: On bulk mutations, continue past per-item failures/conflicts instead of stopping at the first (default: false)

limit.description: Maximum number of items to process per page (default: 100)

max-changes.description: Max write operations a single mutation may apply before refusing (-1 = default: 500, or 50 under --agent) (default: -1)

max-failures.description: With --continue-on-error, stop after this many failures (0 = unlimited) (default: 0)

yes.description: Skip confirmation prompts (for agents and scripts) (default: false)

name: vault_audit
description: Audit managed vault notes without writing

args.description: Additional positional arguments to append to the command. Raw CLI flags are rejected.

name: vault_conflicts
description: List unresolved Zotero note write-back conflicts

args.description: Additional positional arguments to append to the command. Raw CLI flags are rejected.

name: vault_pull
description:
Pull edits made to a managed Zotero child note back into the vault note's
"## Notes" region. This is fast-forward only: a note is updated only when its
local region is unchanged since the last sync. If both the local region and the
remote note changed, it is reported as a conflict and nothing is merged or
overwritten (resolve by hand, or 'vault resolve --keep-vault' to keep the vault
copy). Only notes in the shape this CLI writes are pulled.

Use --dry-run to preview without writing to the vault.

allow-destructive.description: Allow irreversible operations (merge, permanent delete, empty-trash) to apply (default: false)

args.description: Additional positional arguments to append to the command. Raw CLI flags are rejected.

continue-on-error.description: On bulk mutations, continue past per-item failures/conflicts instead of stopping at the first (default: false)

max-changes.description: Max write operations a single mutation may apply before refusing (-1 = default: 500, or 50 under --agent) (default: -1)

max-failures.description: With --continue-on-error, stop after this many failures (0 = unlimited) (default: 0)

yes.description: Skip confirmation prompts (for agents and scripts) (default: false)

name: vault_push
description:
Push the user-owned Notes region of each vault note to a single managed Zotero
child note (Obsidian -> Zotero). Reads stay local; writes go to the Web API (an
api_key must be configured). Idempotent and conflict-safe: a note is only written
when its Notes region changed since the last push, the remote note body is checked
first, and a divergent remote is never overwritten — a conflict artifact is
written under _vault-zotero-conflicts/ and reported instead.

Use --dry-run to preview create/update/conflict without writing anything.

allow-destructive.description: Allow irreversible operations (merge, permanent delete, empty-trash) to apply (default: false)

args.description: Additional positional arguments to append to the command. Raw CLI flags are rejected.

continue-on-error.description: On bulk mutations, continue past per-item failures/conflicts instead of stopping at the first (default: false)

max-changes.description: Max write operations a single mutation may apply before refusing (-1 = default: 500, or 50 under --agent) (default: -1)

max-failures.description: With --continue-on-error, stop after this many failures (0 = unlimited) (default: 0)

yes.description: Skip confirmation prompts (for agents and scripts) (default: false)

name: vault_resolve
description:
Resolve a conflict (or a remotely-deleted note) by choosing a direction:

--keep-vault writes the vault's Notes region over the Zotero child note, using
the live remote version as the precondition. --recreate re-creates a child note
that was deleted in Zotero.

--keep-remote does the reverse: it pulls the live Zotero note body over the
vault Notes region, discarding local edits (a forced 'vault pull'). Only notes
in the shape this CLI writes are pulled.

Exactly one direction is required so the destructive side is explicit; the
resolved conflict artifact is removed on success.

allow-destructive.description: Allow irreversible operations (merge, permanent delete, empty-trash) to apply (default: false)

args.description: Additional positional arguments to append to the command. Raw CLI flags are rejected.

continue-on-error.description: On bulk mutations, continue past per-item failures/conflicts instead of stopping at the first (default: false)

keep-remote.description: Pull the Zotero note over the vault Notes region (discards local edits) (default: false)

keep-vault.description: Write the vault copy over the Zotero note (required direction) (default: false)

max-changes.description: Max write operations a single mutation may apply before refusing (-1 = default: 500, or 50 under --agent) (default: -1)

max-failures.description: With --continue-on-error, stop after this many failures (0 = unlimited) (default: 0)

recreate.description: Re-create a child note deleted in Zotero (default: false)

yes.description: Skip confirmation prompts (for agents and scripts) (default: false)

name: vault_sync
description:
Materialize literature notes into an Obsidian or Logseq vault from the local
store (run 'sync' first). Notes are named from the citation key, carry zotero://
backlinks, and embed current annotations in a managed block.

Re-running is idempotent and non-destructive: only the managed frontmatter keys
and the fenced annotations block change; your prose and other frontmatter keys
are preserved. Use --dry-run to preview create/update/unchanged without writing.

allow-destructive.description: Allow irreversible operations (merge, permanent delete, empty-trash) to apply (default: false)

args.description: Additional positional arguments to append to the command. Raw CLI flags are rejected.

collection.description: Only sync items in this collection key

continue-on-error.description: On bulk mutations, continue past per-item failures/conflicts instead of stopping at the first (default: false)

format.description: Note format: obsidian or logseq (default: obsidian)

item-type.description: Only sync items of this type

limit.description: Maximum items to sync (0 = all) (default: 0)

max-changes.description: Max write operations a single mutation may apply before refusing (-1 = default: 500, or 50 under --agent) (default: -1)

max-failures.description: With --continue-on-error, stop after this many failures (0 = unlimited) (default: 0)

tag.description: Only sync items with this tag

yes.description: Skip confirmation prompts (for agents and scripts) (default: false)

name: workflow_archive
description:
Archive fetches all syncable resources from the API and stores them in a
local SQLite database. Supports incremental sync (only new data since last run)
and full resync. After archiving, use 'search' for instant full-text search.

allow-destructive.description: Allow irreversible operations (merge, permanent delete, empty-trash) to apply (default: false)

continue-on-error.description: On bulk mutations, continue past per-item failures/conflicts instead of stopping at the first (default: false)

full.description: Full re-archive (ignore previous sync state) (default: false)

max-changes.description: Max write operations a single mutation may apply before refusing (-1 = default: 500, or 50 under --agent) (default: -1)

max-failures.description: With --continue-on-error, stop after this many failures (0 = unlimited) (default: 0)

yes.description: Skip confirmation prompts (for agents and scripts) (default: false)

name: workflow_status
description: Show local archive status and sync state for all resources

name: workflow_submit
description: Submit an inline multi-step workflow executed by zotio's transactional runner — previews unless yes; one approval, one journal run id; steps validated per-command exactly like command_run.

continue_on_error.description: Continue executing later steps after a step fails.

args.description: Positional arguments only.

command.description: Mirrorable command path, such as items enrich.

stdin_from.description: Name of an earlier step whose output becomes stdin.

yes.description: Apply the full workflow once instead of previewing it.
